text: ETCS shall provide the driver with information to allow safe train operation.
ETCS shall supervise train and shunting movements.
If supervision is performed by a Radio Block Centre (RBC), ETCS shall prevent traction unit movements not authorized by the RBC.
ETCS shall be functional to a maximum train speed of 500 km/h.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall indicate the current application level on the Driver-Machine Interface (DMI).
ETCS shall apply the brake if the driver fails to acknowledge a level transition requested from trackside.
ETCS shall be compatible with existing national systems listed in the Control-Command and Signalling (CCS) Technical Specification for Interoperability (TSI).
The ETCS trainborne equipment shall supervise operational states including Full Supervision and Shunting.
ETCS shall initiate a brake application if the driver fails to acknowledge a required state transition.
ETCS shall initiate a brake application if an equipped train passes a level transition to a level for which it is not equipped.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall indicate the current operational status on the DMI.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall use default national values if no valid values for the current location are available.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall perform an automatic self-test at start-up.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall indicate the self-test result on the DMI.
ETCS shall require train data entry before allowing train movement.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall allow manual data entry or overwriting only when the train is stationary.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall store current train data until the traction unit is no longer operative.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall supervise shunting operation to a permitted national speed value.
ETCS shall provide a train trip function if a shunting movement passes a stop signal.
ETCS shall calculate the permitted speed for all locations of the authorized movement.
ETCS shall calculate an emergency braking curve and a service braking curve based on relevant data.
The ETCS trainborne equipment shall determine the location of the entire train.
The ETCS trainborne equipment shall transmit the location of the entire train to the RBC on lines fitted with RBC.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall display the actual speed on the DMI.
ETCS shall provide visual and acoustic warnings to the driver at least 5 seconds before a braking intervention.
ETCS shall monitor the direction of movement to protect against roll away and unwanted reverse movements.
The ETCS trainborne equipment shall record all data entered or received and all data indicated to the driver.
ETCS shall trigger the emergency brake when a traction unit passes a stop signal without authorization.
The ETCS trainborne equipment shall transmit its train identification to the RBC.
The ETCS onboard equipment shall display plain text messages received from trackside.
